Localization.Description: Password spraying in MongoDB. Password spraying is a type of brute-force attack in which an attacker tries the most popular passwords sequentially for each user, which helps avoid blocking the accounts of the attacked users.

EventDescriptions.EventDescription: Password spraying attempts from host {src.ip} on host {event_src.ip}. Number of login attempts: {count.subevents}.
Successful password spraying from host {src.ip} on host {event_src.ip}. Attackers obtained the password of user {subject.account.name}.
Successful password spraying from host {src.ip} on host {event_src.ip}. Attackers obtained the passwords of the following users: {datafield2}. Number of failed login attempts: {count.subevents}.
